slides.title: The 3-Step Strategy
slides.text:
To solve any Venn Diagram problem correctly, follow these three steps:

1. List the members (if hidden).
2. Fill the Intersection first.
3. Subtract to find the 'Only' regions.

slides.title: Step 1: Decoding the List
slides.text:
Sometimes sets are hidden in algebra!

Set A = { x : 2 < x < 6 }
This means 'integers between 2 and 6'.
List: { 3, 4, 5 }

slides.title: Step 2: Finding the Intersection
slides.text:
Let's compare two sets:
A = { 3, 4, 5 }
B = { 4, 5, 6 }

What is in BOTH? { 4, 5 }.
We place these in the middle immediately.

slides.title: Step 3: Calculating 'Only'
slides.text:
Set A has {3, 4, 5}. But {4, 5} are already in the middle!

So, A Only gets the remaining member: 3.
Similarly, B Only gets 6.

slides.title: Strategy: Working with Counts
slides.text:
Sometimes exam questions give you Totals instead of lists.

"n(P) = 20. Intersection = 5. Find n(P Only)."

Rule: Whole Circle - Middle = Only.
20 - 5 = 15.

slides.title: Strategy: The Universal Set
slides.text:
Question: "Total pupils = 40. Union = 35. Find the Complement."'

Complement means the Outside region.
Total - Union = Outside.
40 - 35 = 5.
